description: Issue #205 Cat 5: Character deduplication for overlapping glyphs

userStories.title: Implement character deduplication
userStories.description: Python pdfplumber deduplicates characters at nearly identical positions (overlapping glyphs from form fields, copy-paste artifacts, or shadow/embossed effects). pdfplumber-rs extracts all glyphs independently without deduplication. Implement a char deduplication step that removes duplicate chars before word grouping, matching Python's behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Chars with same text and (x0, top) within tolerance (~1.0 pt) are deduplicated
Deduplication is configurable (enabled by default, can be disabled)
No regression on PDFs without duplicate chars
userStories.notes: Key files: crates/pdfplumber-core/src/text.rs, crates/pdfplumber/src/page.rs. Python pdfplumber's dedupe logic: chars are duplicates if same text content and (x0, top) within tolerance (default 1 unit). Dedup should happen after char extraction but before word grouping. Implementation approach: sort chars by (x0, top), then linear scan to remove duplicates within tolerance. Add a `dedupe_tolerance: Option<f64>` to extraction options. Check Python source: pdfplumber/utils/text.py for exact algorithm.
